tests.title: Quantitative Aptitude Prep
tests.description: Covers essential quantitative aptitude topics including Profit & Loss, Percentages, and Probability.

questions.question: A shopkeeper sells an article for $180 at a gain of 20%. What was the cost price of the article?
If log 27 = 1.431, what is the value of log 9?
Three unbiased coins are tossed. What is the probability of getting at most two heads?

tests.title: Technical Web Core Assessment
tests.description: Tests fundamental concepts of modern Full-Stack web development (React, JavaScript, CSS).

questions.question: Which of the following is true about React state updates?
options: They directly modify the component state on the spot
They may be batched and are asynchronous

questions.question: What is the purpose of the useEffect dependency array in React?
options: To specify when the effect function should run or re-trigger

placementDrives.eligibility: B.Tech / MCA / M.Sc with CGPA >= 7.5. No standing backlogs.
Computer Science, Information Technology, or related streams.
Highly proficient in Mathematics / Stats. CGPA >= 8.5.

trainingResources.title: Acing the Coding Technical Interview
trainingResources.content: Understand Big O notation, practice Arrays, Hashing, Linked Lists, Trees, and DP. Always communicate your thoughts out loud during tech interviews.

trainingResources.title: Formulas and Tricks for Quantitative Aptitude
trainingResources.content: Percentages: % change = (diff / original) * 100. Profit & Loss: Profit = SP - CP. Speed & Distance: Relative speed = Sum of speeds (moving in opposite directions).

trainingResources.title: Behavioral Questions Guide (STAR Method)
trainingResources.content: STAR stands for Situation, Task, Action, Result. Frame all response stories around how you resolved conflicts, solved tech bugs, or managed tight schedules.
